title: 以下、Kernel/FUGUEに活用できる？ Server: SimpleHTTP/0.6 Python/3.11.9
summary_text: Findings: - `routerChoice=sonnet` with `delegationScript` empty is **strong suspicion**, but not by itself definitive proof that the Claude worker lane is unavailable. - It shows a routing decision without an executable handoff in this path, which is an execution-contract gap and a high risk for silent no-op/fallback behavior. - You still need one bridge-authoritative execution failure signal (for example `no_route`, `unsupported`, `auth_unavailable`, or repeated null executor on explicit Claude-targeted probes in the same run). Recommended stop condition: - Mark `Claude lane unavailable` and `BLOCKED` when an explicit Claude-targeted probe through the same external router returns `routerChoice=sonnet` with `delegationScript=null/empty` (or equivalent no-executor error) for **3 consecutive attempts in the current run**, and no alternate Claude executor is advertised by the router capability/contract output.
